data.text: the edible blue or blackish berry of any of several North American plants (genus Vaccinium) of the heath family; also: a low or tall shrub producing these berries
a variable moisture-loving North American shrub (Vaccinium corymbosum) that is the source of most cultivated blueberries; also: its fruit

a low-growing deciduous shrub (Vaccinium uliginosum) of the heath family that grows in cool temperate regions of North America and Eurasia and has pink to white urn-shaped flowers and blue to bluish-black edible berries; also: the berry of the alpine bilberry

a low-growing deciduous shrub (Vaccinium uliginosum) of the heath family that grows in cool temperate regions of North America and Eurasia and has pink to white urn-shaped flowers and blue to bluish-black edible berries; also: the berry of the bog bilberry
